title: Introduction
description: NOC 63220 – Shoe repairers and shoemakers | GTR Immigration In an increasingly sustainability-conscious Canadian economy, NOC 63220 (Shoe repairers and shoemakers) professionals play a vital role in the "repair and reuse" movement. Beyond standard maintenance, these skilled artisans are responsible for the custom fabrication of orthopedic footwear and the restoration of luxury leather goods. As a TEER 3 occupation, this trade offers unique opportunities for self-employment and specialized entry into the Canadian labor market, especially in urban centers with high demand for premium and medical footwear services.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level3Major Group63 – Occupations in sales and servicesSub-Major Group632 – Occupations in servicesMinor Group6322 – Other specialized service occupationsNOC Code63220

duties: Discuss footwear specifications and style with customers and take measurements.
Select patterns and leather or other materials and outline patterns on material.
Cut parts and sew them together or glue them to form shoe uppers.
Attach insoles to shoe lasts and join uppers to insoles.
Repair shoes and other leather articles by replacing soles and heels.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Some college or other specialized courses in shoemaking or shoe repair are often required.
Several years of on-the-job training or apprenticeship are mandatory.
job_outlook: The outlook is Stable. Replacement needs due to retirements will account for the majority of job openings. Skilled workers who can combine traditional repair with modern orthopedic tech will have the strongest prospects.
labour_market_demand: The demand for shoemakers and repairers is steady but highly specialized. There is a growing niche for orthopedic shoemakers to support Canada's aging population and healthcare system. Because fewer young Canadians are entering this traditional trade, there is a distinct opening for experienced international craftspersons to fill vacancies in established repair shops and custom boutiques.
Artisanal Resurgence: High appreciation for handcrafted and sustainable goods in major cities.
Entrepreneurial Potential: High success rate for independent shop owners.
Specialized Healthcare Roles: Opportunities to work with podiatrists and health clinics on orthopedic solutions.
pathways: Express Entry (Federal Skilled Trades): Eligible as a TEER 3 skilled trade.
PNP Skilled Trades Stream: Provinces like Ontario and BC often target specialized trades.
Self-Employed Persons Program: For world-class shoemakers with a proven independent track record.
